question: What is a line of symmetry?
options: A line that divides a shape into two unequal parts
A line that divides a shape into two equal mirror-image halves
A line drawn at the centre of a circle
explanation: A line of symmetry (axis of symmetry) divides a figure into two identical halves that are mirror images of each other when folded along that line.
subject: Mathematics

question: How many lines of symmetry does an equilateral triangle have?
explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry — one from each vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side. This is because all three sides and angles are equal.
subject: Mathematics

question: How many lines of symmetry does a circle have?
explanation: A circle has infinitely many lines of symmetry because any diameter (a line through the centre) divides it into two equal semicircles.
subject: Mathematics

question: Which letter of the English alphabet has a vertical line of symmetry?
explanation: The letter 'A' has a vertical line of symmetry — fold it vertically through the middle and both halves match. F, G and Z have no lines of symmetry.
subject: Mathematics

question: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: 2 along the diagonals and 2 through the midpoints of opposite sides.
subject: Mathematics

question: A rectangle (non-square) has how many lines of symmetry?
explanation: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry — one horizontal (through midpoints of longer sides) and one vertical (through midpoints of shorter sides). Its diagonals are NOT lines of symmetry.
subject: Mathematics

question: Which of the following figures has NO line of symmetry?
explanation: A scalene triangle has all sides of different lengths and all angles of different measures, so it has no line of symmetry. All other figures listed have at least one.
subject: Mathematics

question: When you look at your reflection in a mirror, your right hand appears as your left hand. This is an example of:
explanation: A mirror reflection creates a mirror image where left and right are interchanged. This is mirror symmetry or reflection symmetry — the mirror acts as the line of symmetry.
subject: Mathematics

question: A regular hexagon has how many lines of symmetry?
explanation: A regular hexagon has 6 lines of symmetry — 3 connecting opposite vertices and 3 connecting midpoints of opposite sides. A regular polygon with n sides has n lines of symmetry.
subject: Mathematics

question: An isosceles triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
explanation: An isosceles triangle has exactly 1 line of symmetry — the perpendicular bisector of the base (the line from the apex vertex to the midpoint of the unequal side).
subject: Mathematics
